statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input contains two lines. The first line contains a single integer N, the number of cookies. The second line contains N space-separated integers, representing the sizes of the cookies.
statement.outputFormat: Output contains N lines representing the distribution of the cookies, with each line in the format 'A B' where A and B are the indices of the two children who eat the cookie.

hints.text: This is a classic greedy algorithm problem where we try to distribute cookies between children to maximize the happiness of each pair. We use a greedy approach and iterate through the available cookies in the order of their size.

editorial.title: Fair Distribution of Cookies Editorial
editorial.intuition: Intuition is to always pick the smallest cookie and pair it with the next largest cookie
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution is to use a greedy algorithm that selects the smallest cookie and pairs it with the next largest available cookie, maximizing the happiness of each pair.
